_description: Accumulated intelligence from each agent run. The agent reads this at the start of every run and appends to it at the end. This is the self-learning loop — each run builds on all previous runs.

api_quirks_discovered: minValue parameter on /transactions is accepted but silently ignored — does NOT filter by transaction value
Global /transactions only returns most recent 100 txs network-wide — useless for whale detection
Per-account /accounts/{addr}/transactions is the only reliable way to find large transactions
Binance hot wallet (erd1ylw...) returned 0 txs in 7 days — may use internal transfers that don't show as standard txs
Zero-nonce whale wallets (495K, 495K, 346K EGLD) literally have no transaction history at all — pure cold storage receiving via internal transfers or genesis
data_gaps: No way to query all transactions above a value threshold network-wide
Cannot determine when an account was last active without fetching its transactions
Exchange flow analysis limited to balance snapshots since we can't see all historical inflows/outflows in one API call

what_worked: Entity resolution via known-addresses.json — correctly labeled all major exchanges and DeFi contracts
Per-account transaction queries found 28 whale txs that global endpoint missed entirely
UPbit showed net outflow of ~170K EGLD in the period — the biggest whale signal, only visible via per-account queries
Staking concentration HHI calculation gives a single meaningful number (0.018 = competitive)
what_needs_improvement: Dormant wallet detection not yet implemented — need to compare account timestamps
Exchange flow is currently balance-snapshot-only — should also sum individual tx values for precision
No new token detection yet — need to filter tokens by creation timestamp
DeFi TVL tracking requires querying individual protocol contracts for balances
Anomaly z-scores need at least 4 weeks of data to be statistically meaningful
surprising_findings: DRX (DoctorX) has 2.47M holders but near-zero value — airdrop/spam distribution, not real adoption
xExchange daily volume only $210K across 541 pairs — most pairs are essentially dead
Binance holds 64.8% of all exchange EGLD — extreme single-exchange dependency
48.1% of all EGLD is staked — nearly half the supply is illiquid

methodology_changes: ESTABLISHED: Per-account whale detection as primary method (query top accounts + known exchanges individually)
ESTABLISHED: Baseline snapshot format in data/previous.json for WoW deltas
ESTABLISHED: Known addresses entity resolution pipeline

new_addresses_discovered.reason: Received 170K EGLD from UPbit in multiple tranches (77K, 58K, 35K). Likely UPbit cold storage or OTC counterparty. Should be tracked.

recommendations_for_next_run: Implement dormant wallet detection by checking account timestamps against 180-day threshold
Add new token detection by filtering /tokens for creation timestamps within the report period
Query Hatom liquid staking contract (erd1qqqq...4gzf...) balance directly for sEGLD TVL tracking
Explore /accounts/{addr}/transfers endpoint as alternative to /transactions for more complete flow data
Start building z-score baselines — need 4+ weeks before anomaly detection is meaningful
Investigate the UPbit outflow destination address — if it keeps receiving, it's likely an OTC desk or cold wallet

api_quirks_discovered: Persisted output files from tool results contain a metadata header before the JSON — must find first '[' character before parsing

Provider total locked (11.17M EGLD from /providers) differs significantly from /economics staked (14.25M) — difference is direct node staking, not via delegation contracts. These are different metrics.
MEXC balance grew +8.8% but sampled transactions only showed tiny flows — large exchange transfers likely go through internal or smart contract mechanisms, not standard transactions
coingecko simple/price endpoint worked without rate limiting when called once per run
Per-account query window (7 days ago = 1743894000) correctly captures 5-day period from Apr 1–7; need to verify exact UNIX timestamps each run
data_gaps: Cannot easily trace MEXC internal transfer source — requires deeper subaccount investigation
Cannot query 'who funded Unknown Whale I' without iterating through many potential sender accounts
Exchange internal sweeps between their own wallets are invisible to standard tx queries

what_worked: UPbit OTC pattern confirmed second week in a row — same address receiving large outflows
Exchange balance diff table caught MEXC +8.8% growth that wouldn't have been obvious from txs alone
Wallet balance change table correctly identified Unknown Whale I (+14.2%) as the biggest unreported movement
Staking HHI cross-check: 0.0183 unchanged from baseline, confirms stable competitive market
Previous.json comparison correctly revealed that MEXC in run #1 was reported as 83,519 EGLD (not 165K — that was a different unlabeled account)
what_needs_improvement: Need direct query on Unknown Whale I (erd1vd76) to find funding source
Should add Coinbase secondary wallet (erd1m9qn...) to exchange balance tracking
EmoryaSportsX $23M market cap anomaly needs verification against actual DEX liquidity
WrappedTAO tracking would be valuable given Bittensor cross-chain interest
Z-score baselines need 2 more runs before statistical anomaly detection is meaningful
surprising_findings: Unknown Whale I gained +18,167 EGLD (+14.2%) in one week — no obvious source visible in sampled exchange data
WEGLD holder count declined for first time (134,315 → 134,289, -26) while MEX and USDC both gained holders
MultiversX Community Delegation grew +6.7% in one week — largest single-week staking growth among tracked contracts
EmoryaSportsX claimed $23.2M market cap despite only 10K holders — likely custom price feed artifact, not real market depth
WrappedTAO (Bittensor) at $307/token with 1,287 holders showing real cross-chain AI token interest on MultiversX

methodology_changes: CONFIRMED: Previous.json balance comparison is the primary exchange flow signal — WoW delta table is more reliable than trying to sum individual transactions
ESTABLISHED: Separate 'exchange_balances' section in previous.json for clean WoW exchange flow tracking
ADDED: watch_addresses section to previous.json for persistent address monitoring
DISCOVERED: Provider total locked (from /providers) ≠ staked from /economics — document this distinction clearly
ADDED: BTC and ETH prices to economics snapshot for macro context

new_addresses_discovered.reason: Gained +18,167 EGLD (+14.2%) in one week to 146,011 EGLD. Nonce 8,122 = active account. No exchange label. Funding source unknown.

recommendations_for_next_run: Query erd1vd76...z0 (Unknown Whale I) transactions to find funding source
Query erd1v6x9...hcxnk5 outflows — does it only receive or does it also send? If it sends, to where?

Try /accounts/{addr}/transfers endpoint as alternative to /transactions for MEXC to catch internal transfers
Start tracking WrappedTAO (Bittensor bridge activity) as a cross-chain sentiment indicator
Next run will have 3 data points — begin building mean/stddev baselines for z-score anomaly detection

api_quirks_discovered: mex/tokens volume24h field returns $0 for all tokens despite volume being visible in mex/pairs — use mex/pairs for volume data, not mex/tokens
Per-account /transactions correctly captured the Binance Staking 470K intermediary routing in a single query
mex/pairs tradesCount24h is available and useful for spotting thin liquidity pairs
UPbit OTC desk (erd1v6x9) completed its cycle in 3 weeks: accumulated for 2 weeks, distributed in week 3
data_gaps: Still cannot determine exact Gate.io outflow destination without querying their historical transactions
Cannot see Coinbase custody transfer destination without more detailed tx query
mex/tokens volume data appears unreliable (all zeros) — needs investigation

Exchange balance diff table revealed Gate.io's 25% single-week reduction, which no tx query would have surfaced
UPbit OTC desk tracking paid off in week 3 — the reversal pattern (50K back to UPbit + smaller distributions) confirms it was a 3-week institutional accumulation/distribution cycle
Coinbase → Unknown Whale B connection found by querying the whale's transactions, revealing two Coinbase wallets as sources
what_needs_improvement: Should query /accounts/{addr}/transfers as backup for exchanges with 0 visible transactions (Binance Hot 1 still shows 0 txs)
Still need to investigate erd1z7fnqf4... (new top-50, nonce 4777) transactions to understand what it does
mex/tokens volume data unreliable — report notes this but need alternative DEX volume source
Unknown Whale I (erd1vd76) losing -7810 EGLD in small outflows — still don't know what this wallet does
surprising_findings: Binance Staking lost 470K EGLD but all of it was immediately re-staked — total staked EGLD actually jumped +451K (+3.17%) this week
Staked ratio hit 49.49% — within 0.5pp of the 50% psychological milestone after this week's jump
Every major token by holder count DECLINED for second consecutive week — across 10 tokens ranging from -210 to -441 holders
Gate.io reduced holdings by 25.1% in one week — unusual speed of reduction for a major exchange
UPbit OTC desk pattern completed: accumulated ~170K over 2 weeks, distributed back in week 3 — total cycle ~3 weeks
KuCoin grew +18.4% in balance (+5,378 EGLD) — possible restocking or internal transfer

methodology_changes: CONFIRMED: Intermediary wallet investigation is valuable — always check nonce and immediate txs of recipients of large transfers to classify as routing vs. holding
ADDED: mex/tokens volume24h is unreliable (returns $0) — use mex/pairs for volume data exclusively
ESTABLISHED: When exchange shows 0 tx in period, use balance delta as the primary signal (not tx data)

new_addresses_discovered.reason: New top-50 entrant with 62,431 EGLD, nonce 4,777 (highly active). No label in known-addresses. Could be trading firm, market maker, or large holder.
Intermediary wallet used by Binance Staking to route 470K EGLD to delegation contract. Nonce 3 = purpose-built. Current balance ~2.5 EGLD.

action_items_completed: Queried Unknown Whale I (erd1vd76) transactions — found it losing 7,810 EGLD in multiple small outflows this week. Still no clear label.
Queried UPbit OTC desk (erd1v6x9) — confirmed reversal, sent 50K back to UPbit and smaller amounts to unknown addresses
Added Coinbase secondary (erd1m9qn) to exchange balance tracking — confirmed it sent 8,564 EGLD to Unknown Whale B this week
Began building 3-run baseline for metrics: we now have 3 data points for staked EGLD (14.25M, 14.26M, 14.71M), price ($3.71, $3.71, $3.74), and DEX volume ($78K, $79K, $93K)
recommendations_for_next_run: Query erd1z7fnqf4... transactions to identify what this 4,777-nonce wallet does — trading bot, DEX activity, or large staker
Query erd1at3jm3... (Unknown Whale B) transactions to see if Coinbase inflows continue — if so, this may be systematic institutional accumulation
Try /accounts/{addr}/transfers endpoint for Binance Hot 1 (erd1ylwu) and Binance Hot 2 to see if it shows more data than /transactions
Investigate Gate.io continued decline — query their transactions to find destination of outflows
With 3 data points now available: compute running mean and 1 stddev for key metrics (price, staked, DEX volume) to enable simple anomaly flags
Check if WrappedTAO holders increased this week — previous was 1,287, this week is 1,261 (down). Is the Bittensor bridge trend reversing?
Monitor whether 50% staked ratio threshold is breached next week — if so, worth noting as a milestone in the report

endpoints_that_failed: /accounts/{addr}/transfers for Binance Hot 1 (erd1ylwu) — returned 0 results despite high nonce. Confirmed limitation: exchanges using internal or SC-mediated transfer mechanisms are invisible to /transfers endpoint.
api_quirks_discovered: mex/pairs field names confirmed: 'baseName'/'quoteName' (not 'baseToken.ticker'), 'volume24h' (not 'volumeUSD24h'), 'totalValue' (not 'liquidityUSD')
Routing wallet nonce 80 (erd1lgdlteq) confirmed as pass-through — medium-nonce wallets can still be pure routing wallets if they have near-zero balance before/after forwarding
Low nonce is NOT always cold storage: Unknown Whale A had nonce 4 for 4+ weeks, then was reactivated. Low nonce = lower probability of activity, not certainty.
Coinbase outflow + simultaneous inflow from other whales in same 72h window strongly suggests Coinbase OTC desk intermediation
data_gaps: Cannot confirm identity of erd18mv2z6r2 (Unknown Mega Whale) — nonce 21 means minimal public activity, no API assets label
Cannot see Binance Hot 1 transaction flows (internal mechanisms), balance delta is the only signal
Gate.io outflow destinations are hundreds of small addresses — labor-intensive to trace, no single large recipient

what_worked: Intermediary wallet investigation again proven valuable: traced Coinbase → erd1lgdlteq (nonce 80 router) → erd18mv2z6r2 correctly using the pattern
Exchange balance diff table caught the Coinbase net-neutral position despite massive gross flows (+514K in, -798K out = only -7K net), which confirmed OTC intermediation hypothesis
OTC coordination confirmation: querying erd1z7fnqf4 transactions revealed same recipient addresses as erd1v6x9, proving the two-wallet OTC network
4-run z-score baselines now available: EGLD price [3.71, 3.71, 3.74, 4.02] mean $3.795 SD $0.150; DEX volume [$78K, $79K, $93K, $186K] mean $109K SD $49K
Bybit's +27.9% gain correctly attributed to being downstream of the UPbit OTC distribution pipeline
what_needs_improvement: Still cannot identify erd18mv2z6r2 — need to query its outflows next week to understand if it's accumulating or distributing
Binance Hot 1 remains opaque — /transactions and /transfers both return 0 results
WrappedTAO declining 4 weeks straight but small sample — consider graduating from active tracking
surprising_findings: Coinbase executed a bilateral OTC deal in 72 hours: received ~1.026M EGLD from Whale A + Whale B simultaneously while distributing 798K to new mega-whale — net only -7K EGLD
Unknown Whale A (nonce 4, classified cold storage for 4 weeks) suddenly reactivated — low nonce is a probability indicator not an absolute
UPbit OTC desk started Cycle 2 within one week of completing Cycle 1 — faster than expected, suggesting ongoing institutional demand
xExchange volume doubled in one week to $186K — first time in 4 weeks it significantly deviated from baseline
ZPAY price +33% WoW with no obvious catalytic event — possible insider accumulation preceding an announcement
erd18mv2z6r2 is now the 4th largest non-system account on MultiversX at 991K EGLD ($3.98M)

methodology_changes: CONFIRMED: Medium-nonce wallets (nonce 80) can still be routing wallets — the diagnostic is near-zero balance before/after, not nonce alone
CONFIRMED: Low nonce ≠ cold storage guarantee. Unknown Whale A (nonce 4) proved this. Nonce is a probability indicator only.
ESTABLISHED: Coinbase OTC pattern — simultaneous large inflows and outflows from different counterparties = OTC intermediation. Net balance change is the signal, not gross flows.
ADDED: mex/pairs field names to API quirks reference — baseName, quoteName, volume24h, totalValue

new_addresses_discovered.reason: Received 798,649 EGLD from Coinbase via routing wallet on Apr 18. Now 4th-largest non-system account. Nonce 21. Identity completely unknown. #1 priority to track.
Routing wallet used by Coinbase to forward 798,649 EGLD to erd18mv2z6r2. Nonce 80. Near-zero balance before and after.

action_items_completed: Queried erd1z7fnqf4 transactions — confirmed OTC coordination with erd1v6x9, distributes to same recipients in same amounts
Tried /accounts/{addr}/transfers for Binance Hot 1 — confirmed 0 results (limitation documented)
Investigated Gate.io outflows — hundreds of small withdrawals (600-1,200 EGLD each) + one 7,500 EGLD outflow, consistent with sustained customer withdrawals
Checked WrappedTAO holders: 1,256 (was 1,261) — continuing slow decline
Applied z-scores now that 4 data points available: DEX volume is 1.6 sigma above mean (anomaly), EGLD price is 1.5 sigma above mean

running_baselines.note: 4 data points now available. Mean and SD are now statistically meaningful for basic anomaly detection.

recommendations_for_next_run: Query erd18mv2z6r2 transactions — does the 991K EGLD stay put, get staked, or get distributed? This is the #1 priority. If it sends anywhere, trace the destination immediately.
Query remaining Unknown Whale A (erd1kn38) — does it continue selling the remaining 143K EGLD? If yes, continued Coinbase inflow pressure.
Investigate the ZPAY +33% price surge — check if there's new protocol activity, an upcoming announcement, or a whale accumulation pattern behind it.
Continue z-score tracking: update baselines with 5th data point. EGLD price and DEX volume both above 1.5 sigma — watch if trend continues.
Gate.io at 35.6K EGLD — query outflows again. If <25K next week, flag as a significant structural event (exchange delisting EGLD or major customer exodus).
WrappedTAO at 1,256 holders (4 consecutive declines from 1,287): graduate to 'established declining trend' and reduce monitoring frequency.
Monitor whether Bybit's +27.9% gain (+112K EGLD) stabilizes or continues — if UPbit OTC cycle 2 continues at current pace, Bybit may receive more inflows over the next 2-3 weeks.

endpoints_that_failed: /tokens?sort=timestamp — Bad Request (400). The accepted sort fields are: accounts, transactions, price, marketCap. Used /transactions to ESDT contract as a workaround for issuance detection.
api_quirks_discovered: tokens endpoint sort field is restricted — must filter by timestamp client-side after pagination, OR query the ESDT system contract for issuance txs directly.
Per-account /accounts/{addr} returns timestamp = last activity. Useful for dormancy classification but does not include transaction history.
mex/pairs has 25 max page size. Total pairs (544) requires multiple pages or sampling top 25.
Z-score threshold |z|>1.5 produces useful "approaching anomaly" flags (EGLD price now +3.29σ vs baseline mean — clear high anomaly).
data_gaps: Newly-issued token detection requires custom workaround (sort by timestamp not supported)
Provider churn precision limited until previous.json stores FULL provider list (currently top 50 only)
Per-address last-active timestamp does not include the prior-period activity window — dormancy detection requires per-wallet historical tx queries

what_worked: Whale tier stratification produced an actionable directional signal (mega contracting, large+mid expanding) — clearer than raw exchange flow alone
Entity netting collapsed Binance's 4 wallets into +39K net which is the right resolution for a directional read
APR distribution histogram pinpoints the asymmetric value zone (4 providers in 9-10% bucket holding 6.5% of stake, all 0% fee) more clearly than the prior leaderboard
Z-score with 5 data points now produces meaningful anomaly classification (EGLD price +3.29σ flagged correctly as high)
Cross-checking two anomalies (price up, DEX volume reverted) yielded a higher-quality insight than either alone — the price move is CEX-driven, not DEX-driven
Per-protocol DeFi breakdown established baseline across 7 protocols — sets up meaningful WoW deltas from next run
what_needs_improvement: Dormant activation detection still relies on flagging wallets with sudden balance changes; should add per-wallet historical-tx queries to verify the prior dormancy period directly
Newly-issued token detection currently requires querying ESDT system contract — should explore /tokens with timestamp filter via client-side pagination of the full set
Validator churn comparison is currently uncalibrated because previous.json had only top 5 providers — next run will have full 50, enabling real churn metrics
Total delegators baseline established (179,038) but no WoW comparison this run
surprising_findings: EGLD price hit a +3.29σ z-score anomaly while DEX volume reverted to baseline — the price rally is decoupled from on-chain activity, signaling CEX/structural drivers
Crypto.com operates a previously-untracked secondary wallet (erd1qr9av) — adds 3K to tracked exchange balances. Likely many more secondary wallets exist for major exchanges that we don't know about
UPbit injected the largest single-week OTC inflow (188K EGLD) — but desk only distributed 94K (50% absorption rate). Cycle 2 is in early-injection phase, distribution coming next 2-3 weeks
Three "cold storage" wallet reactivations in five weeks (Whale A, Whale B, Whale C) — this is now a confirmed structural pattern. Low-nonce as cold-storage proxy is unreliable on MultiversX
xExchange volume reverted from $186K to $95K (-49%) — the +1.6σ spike was a one-week event, not a regime shift
5 zero-fee providers offer ≥9% APR with combined 738K EGLD locked (6.5% of delegated). The asymmetric value zone for delegators has widened — but stake hasn't migrated there in proportion

ESTABLISHED: Z-score with |z|>1.5 "approaching anomaly" tier; |z|>2 medium; |z|>3 high
CONFIRMED: Low-nonce ≠ cold-storage guarantee. Three reactivations in five weeks. This is now a *structural pattern*, not anecdotal.

new_addresses_discovered.reason: Crypto.com secondary wallet — already labeled in known-addresses.json but appearing with 3K EGLD this run. Establishes new tracking baseline.

action_items_completed: Investigated Unknown Mega Whale (erd18mv2z6r2): held position with -6K EGLD movement back to Coinbase routing. Largely stationary.

Tracked Bybit: stable +2K EGLD WoW after last week's +112K spike
Tracked Gate.io: REVERSED its 4-week decline (+33.7% WoW) — flagged in trend_indicators
Newly-issued token check: zero new fungible tokens this period (verified via /transactions to ESDT contract)
Implemented full v2 schema additions (whale_tiers, entity_netting, apr_distribution, apr_outliers, churn, protocol_breakdown, trend_indicators)
recommendations_for_next_run: Query Unknown Whale C (erd1a2t7xu4...0mgd) recent transactions to identify funding source for +86K EGLD inflow
Track UPbit OTC desk distribution rate: this week injected 188K, distributed only 94K (50% absorption). Watch if rate accelerates next week — typical cycle is 3 weeks.
Investigate why Gate.io reversed its 4-week decline — query its inflows for this week to see if it's a customer wave or treasury reload
Add provider-level WoW tracking by storing the FULL provider list in previous.json (currently only top 5 stored — limits churn analysis precision)
Compute z-score for MEX price now that we have 1 baseline data point starting next run
Crypto.com secondary wallet (erd1qr9av) only had 3K — investigate if this is a short-term routing wallet or a longer-term custody position
Investigate 4 providers in 9-10% APR bucket with 0% fees — are these likely to attract delegator migration? Track their delegator counts WoW
Token holder declines now in 5th consecutive week — graduate from "anomaly" to "established structural trend" in trend_indicators consecutive_streaks

api_quirks_discovered: /accounts/{addr}/transfers/count?after={ts} is the correct endpoint for protocol transfers_24h — NOT scrCount or transfersLast24h fields on the account object (those are not present in /accounts/{addr} response).

When searching for Hatom H-tokens, /tokens/{identifier} returns 404 for incorrectly-formatted identifiers; /tokens?search={ticker} returns multiple hits and you can filter for ones starting with 'Hatom' — this is the reliable token discovery path.

Routing wallet detection refined: medium-nonce wallets (147, 169, 180) drained to near-zero (~0 to 5,999 EGLD) immediately after each receipt = pure pass-through. Combined with consistent identical-amount routing (5,999 / 7,999 / 8,000 EGLD chunks), this is the fingerprint of a managed cross-exchange OTC infrastructure.
/accounts/{addr} balance for routing wallets is sometimes near-zero with scientific notation '5999.9994' — must parse correctly without rounding.
data_gaps: Newly-issued token detection still requires workaround — /tokens?sort=timestamp returns 400.
Cannot query Binance Hot 1 (erd1ylw) transactions — only 1 visible (last 7 days). Internal sweeps remain opaque.
Provider full-list churn now possible from next run since we're storing 108 providers in previous.json (was top 50 only).

what_worked: Discovered cross-exchange OTC funnel by tracing routing wallet recipients in OTC Distribution Wallet's transaction list — saw matching 5,999/7,999/8,000 chunks coming from Binance.com 2 within minutes. This is the highest-quality finding of any run.

Yield-chasing migration prediction from run #5 came true: Ninja Staking and Egld Staking Provider were top-2 gainers, exactly the 0%-fee 9%+ APR profile flagged. The asymmetric value zone is now actively pulling capital.

Whale tier stratification flipped this week (mega+large gain, mid loss) vs last week (mega contracting, mid expanding). The reversal makes the directional signal sharper — OTC distribution is going UP the whale tier, not dispersing.

Z-score reversion validated: EGLD price went from +3.29σ (high, last week) to +0.77σ (normal, this week). The agent's 'one-week event' classification was correct — this is the kind of self-correction that builds trust.
Storing FULL provider list (108) in previous.json this run unlocks meaningful churn analysis from run #7.
what_needs_improvement: MEX price baseline still has only N=2; need 2 more weeks for z-score.
Staked ratio baseline only N=2; same gap.
Newly-issued token detection still degraded — need to figure out client-side timestamp filter or a different endpoint.

Could validate the cross-exchange OTC theory by querying the OTC Desk recipients (erd1f4kcxxn4, erd1tuvllxaf, erd1krmy7xld, erd142cjv2r5) to see if THEY also forward to other exchanges' deposit addresses.
Hatom Lending TVL might be missing some H-tokens (ETH versions, isolated lending positions). Should explore the Hatom factory contract for the full deployed-tokens list.
surprising_findings: Binance.com 2 (erd1sdsl) is funding UPbit OTC desks via 3 routing wallets — first observed cross-exchange OTC infrastructure on MultiversX. Volume: 80K+ EGLD this week. This rewrites the OTC narrative: it's shared infrastructure, not exchange-specific.
Two 0%-fee 9%+ APR providers gained the equivalent of what 5 lower-APR providers lost in a single week. After 4 weeks of inertia, the yield-chase finally activated.
Mega whale tier +78K, mid whale tier -78K — almost exactly offsetting. Wealth concentrated upward by ~78K EGLD this week with no net change in whale-tier supply.
JEXchange Lite Pool (erd1272et87...) — first appearance with non-trivial transfer count (2,229 in 24h). Was effectively invisible in prior runs.

Token holder declines now in 6th consecutive week — from anomaly to confirmed structural pattern. Yet active-token base (>$1M mcap) is stable. Implication: airdrop wallet decay, not real adoption decline.
UPbit absorbed +78K EGLD this week, the largest single-wallet gain on the network. Source: 79K returned from OTC desks + customer deposits + 18K from unknown.

methodology_changes: ESTABLISHED: Cross-exchange OTC funnel pattern. Pattern: high-nonce routing wallets (100-200 nonce range) with near-zero balance, receiving identical-amount chunks (5,999/7,999/8,000 EGLD) from one exchange and forwarding within minutes to another exchange's OTC desk. To detect: query OTC desk inflows, then query each upstream sender for upstream pattern.
ESTABLISHED: Yield-chasing migration as a recurring trend indicator. To detect: compare top staking gainers/losers WoW, classify by APR bucket, count net rotation across the 9%+ vs <8% APR boundary.
ADDED: /accounts/{addr}/transfers/count?after={ts} as the correct endpoint for protocol-level transfers_24h. Previous attempts using account object fields returned 0/null.
CONFIRMED: 50% staked ratio is the next observable milestone. Current 49.50% with consistent +0.06pp/week rate of approach → 1-2 weeks until breach.
ADDED: Storing FULL /providers list (108 providers, was top 50) in previous.json — enables proper churn detection from next run.

new_addresses_discovered.reason: Binance→OTC routing wallet. Nonce 169. Receives 8K EGLD from Binance.com 2 (erd1sdsl), forwards to UPbit OTC Desk or OTC Distribution Wallet within minutes. ~40K EGLD routed this week.
Binance→OTC routing wallet. Nonce 180. Same pattern — 8K EGLD chunks from Binance.com 2 to OTC Distribution Wallet. ~24K EGLD routed this week.
Binance→OTC routing wallet. Nonce 147. 5,999 EGLD chunks from Binance.com 2 to UPbit OTC Desk. ~18K EGLD routed this week.
New top-50 entrant with 18,321 EGLD. Nonce 313 (active). Small back-and-forth flows with erd1s9gusy and erd1s9nvc2 — likely small trader/multi-sig. Worth monitoring.

action_items_completed: Queried Unknown Whale C (erd1a2t7) — confirmed flat at 432K, dormant (action item from run 5).

Validated yield-chasing prediction: Ninja Staking +15.5K and Egld Staking Provider +14.2K — both 0%-fee 9%+ APR.
Computed full z-scores: EGLD price reverted to +0.77σ (was +3.29σ) — confirmed last week's 'one-week event' classification.
